title: Battle droid dispenser
content:
{{Top|fa|leg}} {{Device |image=[[File:Battledroiddispener-infobox.png]] |name=Battle droid dispenser |manufacturer= |model= |type=[[Droid dispenser]]''[[Star Wars: Republic Commando]]'' |culture= |creators= |created= |modifier= |destroyed= |discovered= |owners= |locations=*[[Lucrehulk-class battleship/Legends|''Lucrehulk-class'' droid control ships]] *[[Trandoshan dropship]]s *[[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]] |cost= |value= |structure=[[Color/Legends|Beige]] or brown semi-cylinder |size= |material= |length= |width= |height=Over 1.93 [[Meter/Legends|meters]]''[[Star Wars: Republic Commando]]'' depicts [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droid]] [[Droid dispenser|dispensers]] to be taller than [[B1-series battle droid/Legends|B1-series battle droid]]s, which {{SWE|technology|battledroid|battle droid}} establishes as standing at 1.93 [[Meter/Legends|meters]]. |weight= |protection=[[Blaster/Legends|Blasterproof]] |capacity= |range= |inscription= |markings= |purpose=Dispense [[B1-series battle droid/Legends|B1-series battle droid]]s |heritage= |affiliation=[[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] }} {{Quote|What's that?"
"It's a droid dispenser, Oh-Seven. Next time read the briefing.|Sev and Fixer of Delta Squad, upon first encountering a battle droid dispenser|audio=Fixer-ReadBriefing.flac|}} The '''battle droid dispenser''' was a [[B1-series battle droid/Legends|B1-series battle droid]] [[Droid dispenser|dispenser]] used by the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] against the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. Stationed on [[Lucrehulk-class battleship/Legends|''Lucrehulk-class'' droid control ships]], they were moved around on transport lines and could be launched onto enemy [[Starship/Legends|vessels]] using built-in [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsors]]. [[Trandoshan/Legends|Trandoshan]] [[Slavery/Legends|enslavers]] were provided with the devices during joint operations with the Confederacy. [[Delta Squad/Legends|Delta Squad]], an elite [[Squad/Legends|squad]] of Republic [[Clone commando/Legends|clone commandos]], encountered many [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droid]] dispensers during their deployments, such as the opening engagement of the Clone Wars at the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|First Battle of Geonosis]] in [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]]. Other encounters included a [[Mission to the Chaykin Cluster|mission]] to rescue the [[Acclamator-class transgalactic military assault ship/Legends|''Acclamator''-class assault ship]] ''[[Prosecutor]]'' from Separatist and Trandoshan hands in [[21 BBY/Legends|21 BBY]] and a [[Mission to Kashyyyk (Clone Wars)|mission]] to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]] to rescue the [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] [[Chief/Legends|chieftain]] [[Tarfful/Legends|Tarfful]] in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]]. ==Description== {{Quote|Boss, a demo charge should shut it down for good.|Scorch, advising Boss on destroying a battle droid dispenser|audio=Scorch-BattleDroidDispenserDestroy.flac|}} [[File:Good_test_kach.jpg|thumb|right|250px|A B1-Series battle droid standing next to a battle droid dispenser]] Battle droid dispensers stood over 1.93 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] tall and were [[Color/Legends|beige]] or brown-colored. A semi-cylinder–shaped device, it continually dispensed a supply of [[B1-series battle droid/Legends|B1-series battle droid]]s from three circular hatches mounted on its sides. Limited to dispensing up to three [[B-series battle droid/Legends|droids]] at a [[Time/Legends|time]], each deployment took approximately nine [[Standard second|seconds]], faster than the [[B2 battle droid dispenser|super battle droid dispenser's]] deployment of [[B2-series super battle droid/Legends|B2 super battle droids]]. The B1 [[Battle droid/Legends|droids]] were stored in a deactivated, folded position, unfolding into a standing position upon exiting the device's center. The device was resistant to [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] bolts but could be destroyed using [[Explosive charge/Legends|explosive charges]] such as a [[detonation pack]] or through the use of the [[Laser/Legends|laserfire]] of the [[anti-personnel laser cannon]]s of an [[All Terrain Tactical Enforcer/Legends|AT-TE]] [[Walker/Legends|walker]]. The dispensers were also equipped with [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsors]], allowing them to be launched into other [[Starship/Legends|starships]] from the [[Lucrehulk-class battleship/Legends|''Lucrehulk-class'' droid control ships]] aboard which they were held. They were carried on transport lines through the superstructure inside the [[Droid/Legends|droid]] control ships. ==History== ===Early usage in the Clone Wars=== {{Dialogue|01/425|Three-Eight, the droid dispensers will be setting down in seconds.|Scorch|We can lay some traps, clear up a few sight lines…|Boss|They could land anywhere. Let's spread the pain around.|attr=Delta Squad's advisor and commandos Scorch and Boss, discussing how to deal with incoming droid dispensers|src=}} The [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] used battle droid dispensers during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] against the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. During [[Navy/Legends|naval]] battles, the droid dispensers were launched onto Republic ships, deploying B1s to board and overrun them. They could also be placed on landing pads on [[Planet/Legends|planetary]] surfaces. In its joint operations with [[Trandoshan/Legends|Trandoshan]] [[Slavery/Legends|enslavers]], the Confederacy provided the Trandoshans with droid dispensers. [[Delta Squad/Legends|Delta Squad]], an elite [[Squad/Legends|squad]] of Republic [[Clone commando/Legends|clone commandos]], encountered many of the units during their operations. Battle droid dispensers were often used alongside super battle droid dispensers and occasionally [[droideka dispenser]]s. [[File:BattleDroidDispenser-Geonosis.png|thumb|left|300px|Battle droid dispensers on the transport lines of a Separatist core ship]] In [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]],''[[Star Wars: Republic Commando]]'' places the [[Mission to the Chaykin Cluster]] a [[Standard year/Legends|year]] after the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|First Battle of Geonosis]], which ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates to [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]]. at the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|First Battle of Geonosis]] that marked the start of the Clone Wars,{{Film|II}} Separatist [[Core ship/Legends|core ships]] carried a number of droid dispensers. When Delta Squad infiltrated one such ship, the [[Commando/Legends|commandos]] split up to target and incapacitate crucial systems in the vessel. They reunited in a corridor, above which the dispensers were being carried on a transport line. The [[Cloning/Legends|clone]] squad engaged several waves of B1 battle droids and [[Droideka/Legends|droidekas]] in the corridor, and in the resulting [[Lightfight|blaster fire]], the transport line was damaged and dropped a dispenser, which then activated. Upon first encountering it, commando [[Sev/Legends|Sev]] questioned what the item was, to which fellow commando [[RC-1140/Legends|RC-1140]], also known as Fixer, replied by explaining the device's function and scolded Sev for not having read the mission briefing. The commandos then used a detonation pack to destroy the battle droid dispenser. {{Gamemechanics|Star Wars: Republic Commando}} Delta Squad leader [[Boss (clone commando)/Legends|Boss]] subsequently shot down another droid dispenser from the transport line, destroying the dispenser and killing four battle droids in the resultant explosion. {{Endgame}} ===Chaykin Cluster=== [[File:BattleDroidDispenser-RasProsecutor.png|thumb|right|300px|A battle droid dispenser landing in Hangar B of the Republic Assault Ship ''Prosecutor'']] In [[21 BBY/Legends|21 BBY]], Trandoshan enslavers working with the Separatists [[Attack on the Prosecutor|attacked]] the Republic [[Acclamator-class transgalactic military assault ship/Legends|''Acclamator''-class assault ship]] (RAS) ''[[Prosecutor]]'' while it was patrolling the [[Corellian sector/Legends|Corellian sector]] in order to salvage parts from the ship for Separatist uses. Several [[Standard week/Legends|weeks]] later, the ship reappeared in the [[Chaykin Cluster]] and Delta Squad was sent to [[Mission to the Chaykin Cluster|investigate]] its disappearance. When the Trandoshans were notified of Delta Squad's presence, they unloaded the droid dispensers from their [[Dropship/Legends|dropships]]. Republic commando [[Scorch/Legends|Scorch]] attempted to rendezvous with Boss, who was trapped behind a door in a small corridor aboard the ''Prosecutor''. Entering from a connecting hallway behind Boss, an enslaver pushed a droid dispenser into the opposite end of the corridor, with the device subsequently activating, and the clone was forced to hold the droids off while Scorch [[Slicer/Legends|sliced]] the door panel. Several [[Standard minute/Legends|minutes]] later, Scorch succeeded, and the two destroyed the dispenser. When the squad eventually made their way to the ''Prosecutor''{{'s}} [[Hangar/Legends|hangars]], a Separatist droid control ship exited [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] to finish the salvaging job. Delta Squad then set traps around [[Hangar B]], anticipating battle droid dispensers and super battle droid dispensers sent from the enemy ship. When the craft finally landed, Delta Squad fought off the boarding party, placing a demolition charge on the hangar's [[Force field/Legends|force field]] regulator to re-activate the field and stop the enemy advance in Hangar B. The squad moved on to the next hangar to combat additional forces, and as they arrived in [[Hangar C]], they also encountered a deployed battle droid dispenser. {{Gamemechanics|Star Wars: Republic Commando}} While clearing out Separatist forces in Hangar C, a member of Delta Squad sliced a terminal to start a docked [[74-Z speeder bike/Legends|74-Z speeder bike]]. The bike's [[Engine/Legends|engines]] fired up, and it raced forward until it crashed into a droid dispenser, destroying both the dispenser and the [[Speeder bike/Legends|speeder bike]]. {{Endgame}} The squad defeated the incoming invasion force and used a similar tactic to destroy the hangar's regulator. En route to [[Hangar D]], the group encountered several dispensers that had already been deployed. However, [[Advisor/Legends|clone advisor]] [[01/425]] instructed Delta Squad not to engage the dispensers directly, and the commandos took cover at the rear of the hangar as more dispensers landed, including droideka dispensers. The squad then sliced a [[Turbolift/Legends|lift]] terminal to bring an AT-TE walker to the hangar. Once the lift arrived, Boss fired the walker's [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannons]], destroying the dispensers and the regulator. The commandos subsequently boarded the lift, where they contacted [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Talbot]] of the RAS [[Arrestor (Acclamator-class)|''Arrestor'']] for reinforcements. Moving through the maintenance tunnels and overcoming entrenched droid forces, the clones eventually reached the gun control station, where they activated the automatic defenses and saved the ship with the help of Talbot. ===Mission to Kashyyyk=== In [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]],''[[Order 66: A Republic Commando Novel]]'' dates the [[Mission to Kashyyyk (Clone Wars)|Mission to Kashyyyk]] to eighteen [[Standard day/Legends|days]] before the [[Battle of Kashyyyk]], which ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' furthermore places [[Standard month/Legends|months]] into [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]]. Therefore the Mission to Kashyyyk must have also taken place in the same [[Standard year/Legends|year]]. Delta Squad was [[Mission to Kashyyyk (Clone Wars)|dispatched]] to the planet [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]] to rescue the [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] [[Chief/Legends|chieftain]] [[Tarfful/Legends|Tarfful]] and destroy both Separatist supplies and the [[Kachirho Bridge]]. As Delta Squad made its way through a Trandoshan compound, it destroyed a battle droid dispenser on a Separatist launchpad. Eventually, the squad rescued Tarfful and destroyed the bridge, as well as an opposing [[Recusant-class light destroyer/Legends|''Recusant''-class light destroyer]]. Immediately thereafter, a Republic [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] entered the planet's [[Atmosphere/Legends|airspace]], with 01/425 advising them that the [[Battle of Kashyyyk/Legends|Battle of Kashyyyk]] had begun. ==Behind the scenes== [[File:RCcover.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Battle droid dispensers debuted in ''Star Wars: Republic Commando''.]] The battle droid dispenser appeared in the [[2005]] [[LucasArts]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Republic Commando]]''. Prior to the game's release, the device was mentioned on LucasArts.com's ''Star Wars: Republic Commando'' webpage.{{LucasArts|url=games/swrepubliccommando/hud.html|text=''Star Wars Republic Commando''}} In the game, droid dispensers will continue deploying droids until the dispenser itself is destroyed. The "Guerrilla Warfare" trophy can be achieved in the "Infiltration of the Core Ship" mission as part of the Geonosis campaign. It is achieved if the player shoots down a transported droid dispenser and drops it onto at least four enemies. In the "Save the Ship" mission of the RAS ''Prosecutor'' campaign, the player may choose to instruct a team member to slice a console connected to a [[Speeder/Legends|speeder bike]] in Hangar C. Upon slicing the console, the bike will start and then crash into a droid dispenser, allowing the player to gain the "That Model Is A Death Trap" trophy. ==Appearances== *''[[Star Wars: Republic Commando]]'' {{1st}} ==Sources== *[[Star Wars: Republic Commando: Prima Official Game Guide|''Star Wars: Republic Commando'': Prima Official Game Guide]] ==Notes and references== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of battle droid dispensers}} {{Reflist}} ==External links== *{{LucasArts|url=games/swrepubliccommando/hud.html|text=''Star Wars Republic Commando''}} {{1stm}} [[Category:Confederacy military]] [[Category:Droid dispensers]]
